faqs.question: Why are drain flies common in Morningside Heights buildings?
faqs.answer: Morningside Heights has many pre-war and historic buildings with older plumbing systems that can develop organic buildup in drains, creating ideal breeding grounds for drain flies. The neighborhood's density of restaurants, academic facilities, and residential units also increases the likelihood of drain fly issues, especially in basement levels and older bathroom fixtures.

faqs.answer: Our drain fly treatment service in Morningside Heights ranges from $350 to $550, depending on the severity of the infestation, number of drains affected, and building size. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing before beginning any treatment, with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: Are your drain fly treatments safe for Columbia University area residents and pets?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved, eco-friendly products that are safe for families, students, and pets.
